$comment: Reliability and injury desk pack (prefix ri.) - mechanical failures voiced by era (1960s oil-and-metal through 2010s sensors-and-shutdowns, plus SMGP arcade), honest driver-error assessments, retirement-streak coverage, sit-out and medical bulletins, and two measured memoriam variants. Follows 010-core.json format; read-side only.

1960s: The mechanics drained the oil and found the story written in bright metal.
The offending assembly travels home in a wooden crate, to be argued over at the works.
1980s: The broken bits came back to the garage in a box. Nobody spoke over it.
Somewhere tonight a designer is being asked hard questions at high volume.
2010s: The telemetry is already uploaded - somewhere a factory screen is replaying the failure frame by frame.
Overnight, the data will name the guilty part. It always does.
smgp: THE WRENCH CREW SWARMS. THE PART GETS BENCHED. THE LADDER KEEPS SCROLLING.
GARAGE DOORS DOWN. TOOLBOXES OPEN. THE COMEBACK STARTS TONIGHT.
default: In the garage, the failed component is already on the bench, giving up its story in fragments.
The team will strip the car to its last bolt before it turns a wheel again.
Warranties do not exist at this level - only long nights and second guesses.

1960s: The doctors' report is couched in careful language, as such reports are, and the paddock reads between its lines.
Word from the infirmary is guarded but not grave.
2010s: The team's medical update ran to three sentences and was shared a thousand times within the hour.
Further scans are scheduled; further statements are not.
smgp: THE MEDICAL BAY DOORS SLIDE SHUT. THE WHOLE PADDOCK LEANS IN.
HEALING BAR ON SCREEN. IT FILLS SLOWER THAN ANYONE WANTS.
default: The medical team's statement is brief and its tone is calm - which, in this sport, is the good kind of bulletin.
Recovery has its own calendar, and it does not consult the championship's.
The advice from the medical room is the same as it always is: patience, then patience again.

1960s: The works will want the crankcase on the bench before the transporter is cold.
Metal fails; the question is always why, and the answer is always in the metal.
2010s: The failure is already a ticket in the factory system, stamped urgent.
The reliability group does not sleep after days like this; the rigs run all night.
smgp: THE GARAGE LIGHTS STAY ON ALL NIGHT.
EVERY BOLT GETS THE STARE-DOWN TONIGHT.

templates.headline: The machinery gives out under {subject}[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: A mechanical retirement[[?round: from round {round}]], reported from the paddock
templates.summary: {subject} retired[[?round: from round {round}]] when the car failed[[?reason: - {reason}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} It is the oldest ending in the sport: {subject} pressing on, the instruments telling their bad news, and then the long walk back[[?venue: at {venue}]]. The car gave out[[?reason: - {reason}, the mechanics say]], and no urging from the cockpit could argue with it.

templates.headline: BANG. {player's} race goes up in smoke
templates.deck: Mechanical failure ends it[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.summary: {subject} was running when the car quit[[?round: in round {round}]][[?reason: - {reason}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} No warning, no mercy. One moment {subject} is racing, the next the car is coasting[[?venue: at {venue}]] and the marshals are waving it somewhere safe to die[[?reason: - the verdict from the garage reads {reason}]].
sections.reliability: The points {subject} came for are still out on the circuit. {pool:riGarage}

templates.headline: Shutdown: {player's} car switches itself off[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: Alarms, a slowing car, and a very modern retirement
templates.summary: A systems failure ended {player's} race[[?round: in round {round}]][[?reason: - {reason}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The failure arrived as a number before it arrived as a feeling: an alarm on the wheel, a procedure recited over the radio, and {subject} rolling to a stop[[?venue: at {venue}]] in a car that had already decided the argument[[?reason: - preliminary word points to {reason}]].
sections.reliability: {pool:riGarage} The part flies back to the factory tonight; the explanation will land before the freight does.

templates.headline: SMOKE ON THE CIRCUIT! {player's} machine calls it quits
templates.deck: MECHANICAL K.O.[[?venue: AT {venue}]]
templates.summary: {player's} machine broke[[?round: in round {round}]] and the day ended early.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} THE ENGINE NOTE WOBBLES, THE CROWD GROANS, AND {player's} DAY IS DONE[[?venue: AT {venue}]]. NO CONTINUE SCREEN FOR MACHINERY - WHEN IT BREAKS, IT BREAKS.

templates.headline: Twice is a trend: another failure strands {subject}
templates.deck: {streak} non-finishes in a row, and the garage knows the difference between bad luck and a problem
templates.summary: {subject} retired again[[?round: in round {round}]] - now {streak} retirements in succession.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} One breakage is an anecdote. {streak} in a row is evidence. {player's} car failed again[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?reason: - {reason} this time]], and the word 'again' is doing heavy work in every sentence spoken in that garage tonight.
sections.stats: Reliability is arithmetic: every non-finish is points surrendered at a rate no driving can buy back. The championship does not record excuses, only zeros.

templates.headline: Cruel to the last: {player's} car fails at the season finale
templates.deck: The last day of the year ends in the garage
templates.summary: {player's} season closed with a mechanical failure at the final round[[?venue: at {venue}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Seasons are supposed to end with a flag. {player's} ended with a tow rope: the car broke at the last round of the year[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?reason: - {reason}]], and there is no next weekend to make it right.
sections.context: A winter is a long time to sit with a failure. The one mercy of a finale breakdown is that everyone else's season is over too.

templates.headline: Broken before it began: {subject} retires from the season opener
templates.deck: A winter of work undone in an afternoon
templates.summary: {player's} new season opened with a mechanical retirement[[?venue: at {venue}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Every winter promise met the same afternoon: {player's} car failed at the very first round of the year[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?reason: - {reason}]], and a garage that spent months preparing for this day packed it away early.
sections.reliability: Openers are the sport's honesty test - the round where winter's claims meet a stopwatch. {team} left with a data set instead of a result. {pool:riGarage}
sections.close: The season is long. Tonight, that is both the consolation and the sentence.

templates.headline: No excuses offered: {subject} out through driver error
templates.deck: An honest mistake, honestly owned
templates.summary: {subject} retired after a driving error[[?round: in round {round}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The stewards will not need long and neither will the driver: {subject} got it wrong[[?venue: at {venue}]], the car went where physics sent it, and the afternoon ended in gravel and honesty.
sections.context: There is a difference between a driver who blames the tools and one who reads the moment back plainly. The second kind tends to make the same mistake exactly once.

templates.headline: An error of judgement ends {player's} race
templates.deck: The driver, not the machine, at fault[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.summary: {subject} retired after a driving error[[?round: in round {round}]], the car sound until the moment it was not.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The report is a plain one: {subject} asked the car a question it could not answer[[?venue: at {venue}]] and paid the customary price. The machinery bore no blame; the paddock's sympathy is of the quieter kind reserved for self-inflicted wounds.
sections.context: The old hands hold that a driver learns the true dimensions of a corner only by exceeding them once. By that measure, today counted as education, expensively bought.

templates.headline: One bad input: {subject} crashes out[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: The data will name the corner; the driver already has
templates.summary: {subject} retired from the race after an error[[?round: in round {round}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} By tonight the overlay will show it to the metre: the brake released a fraction early, the line a hand's width wrong, and {player's} race ending at racing speed[[?venue: at {venue}]]. Modern racing leaves no privacy for mistakes.
sections.stats: The margin between committed and crashed at this level is measured in tenths of a degree and hundredths of a second. Today {subject} landed on the wrong side of a number that small.

templates.headline: Pressure tells: {subject} cracks in the fight[[?rival: with {rival}]]
templates.deck: A mistake made at close quarters
templates.summary: {subject} retired after an error made in traffic[[?rival: while racing {rival}]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Mistakes rarely happen in empty air. This one came in the thick of a fight[[?rival: with {rival}]][[?venue: at {venue}]] - the kind of wheel-to-wheel arithmetic where the margin for error is loaned, not owned, and {subject} spent one tenth too many.
sections.rivalry: [[?rival: {rival} drove on. That is the cruelty of these duels: one driver gets the corner, the other gets the walk back. ]]Rivalries are decided as much by the mistakes they force as by the passes they produce.

templates.headline: SPIN AND OUT! {subject} bins it[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: DRIVER ERROR. GAME OVER FOR TODAY.
templates.summary: {subject} spun out of the race[[?round: in round {round}]] - driver error, no arguments.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} FULL SEND, WRONG RESULT. {subject} asked for one slice of grip too many[[?venue: at {venue}]] and the circuit said NO. THE MACHINE IS FINE. THE PRIDE NEEDS A RESPRAY.

templates.headline: The zeros pile up: {streak} straight non-finishes for {subject}
templates.deck: A run that is starting to define the season
templates.summary: {subject} has now retired from {streak} consecutive races.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} There is a moment when a bad patch stops being weather and becomes climate. At {streak} retirements in succession, {player's} season has crossed it - the paddock no longer asks what went wrong this week, only what will go wrong next.
sections.stats: Streaks like this compound: points lost, momentum lost, and a car developed on guesswork because it never runs long enough to learn anything.

templates.headline: Failure analysis: the anatomy of {player's} {streak}-race collapse
templates.deck: A technical reading of a reliability crisis
templates.summary: An engineering view of {player's} {streak} consecutive retirements.

sections.lead: Set sentiment aside and read the pattern like an engineer: {streak} consecutive non-finishes is not misfortune, it is a system producing the same output from the same inputs. Somewhere in {team's} process - design, assembly, operation - a truth is going unheard.
sections.reliability: Components rarely lie. They fail along the load paths they are given, at the margins they are set. {pool:riGarage}
sections.next: The fix will be found the way these fixes are always found: instrumented, itemised, and admitted. Until then, every green light is a hypothesis test.

templates.headline: A season under siege: {streak} straight retirements and counting
templates.deck: This is no longer a rough patch. It is the story of the year.
templates.summary: {player's} retirement run has reached {streak} races.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} Somewhere around the third one it stopped being a slump and became a siege. {streak} consecutive retirements have hollowed out {player's} season - the championship arithmetic wrecked, the garage running on grim routine, the driver rehearsing starts for races the car will not finish.
sections.context: Runs like this test the parts of a team no wind tunnel measures: the willingness to keep bolting the car together carefully when carelessness would be understood.
sections.stats: The ledger makes brutal reading: {streak} rounds, zero finishes, and a points column frozen while every rival's ticks upward.

templates.headline: The hardest laps are the missed ones: {subject} sits out[[?round: round {round}]]
templates.deck: Recovery continues on its own schedule
templates.summary: {subject} was not in the car[[?round: for round {round}]], still recovering.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} There is no lonelier place in the sport than the pit wall during a race you were meant to run. {subject} watched[[?round: round {round}]][[?venue: at {venue}]] from the wrong side of the barrier, the recovery clock still turning.
sections.context: [[?missRaces: The medical schedule shows {missRaces} more rounds before a return can even be discussed. ]]{pool:riBulletin}
sections.close: The stopwatch never asks how you feel. It only asks when you are back.

templates.headline: BENCHED! {subject} sits this one out
templates.deck: RECOVERY MODE[[?round: - ROUND {round} RUNS WITHOUT ITS HEADLINER]]
templates.summary: {subject} missed[[?round: round {round}]] - still healing up.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} THE GRID FORMED UP[[?venue: AT {venue}]] WITH ONE NAME MISSING. {subject} SPENT THE RACE ON THE SIDELINES, HEALING BAR CREEPING RIGHT[[?missRaces: - {missRaces} MORE ROUNDS UNTIL THE RELAUNCH]].

templates.headline: Medical bulletin: {subject} hurt[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?missRaces: , out {missRaces} rounds]]
templates.deck: Injury update[[?round: after round {round}]]
templates.summary: {subject} sustained injuries in an accident[[?missRaces: and is expected to miss {missRaces} races]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The facts first, as the wire prefers them: {subject} was injured in an accident[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?round: during round {round}]]. The driver was attended promptly and is in the care of the medical staff[[?missRaces: - the early estimate is {missRaces} rounds on the sidelines]].
sections.next: [[?missRaces: The comeback date has a number on it now, which is more than most weeks like this get. ]]Further bulletins as the doctors issue them - and not before.
sections.close: The sport holds its breath briefly, then holds its schedule. Both are true at once.

templates.headline: The body pays the bill: {subject} injured[[?venue: at {venue}]]
templates.deck: [[?missRaces:{missRaces} races to heal. ]]A reminder of what the speed costs
templates.summary: {subject} was hurt in a racing accident[[?missRaces: and faces {missRaces} rounds out]].

sections.lead: Racing drivers talk about their bodies the way engineers talk about components: inputs, tolerances, service intervals. Today the toughest component took the hit. {subject} is hurt[[?venue: after an accident at {venue}]], and the machinery of a season suddenly matters less than the person who operates it.
sections.impact: [[?missRaces: {missRaces} rounds is the official estimate, and every driver alive will privately try to beat it. ]]The team's plans bend around an empty seat; the championship does not wait, which is the cruellest thing about it and also the most honest.
sections.close: Cars are rebuilt to a deadline. People are rebuilt to their own.

templates.headline: {subject} hurt in racing accident; recovery expected
templates.deck: A bulletin from the circuit infirmary[[?round: , round {round}]]
templates.summary: {subject} suffered injuries[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?missRaces: and will be absent some {missRaces} rounds]].

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} It falls to this page to report that {subject} came to grief[[?venue: at {venue}]] and has been placed in the doctors' charge. The word from the infirmary is measured: injuries, yes; danger, no[[?missRaces: - an absence of perhaps {missRaces} rounds]].
sections.context: {pool:riBulletin} Such bulletins are read twice in every garage, and the paddock does not exhale until the second reading matches the first.
sections.close: The car is secondary, which is the only correct order of concern.

templates.headline: No more racing this year: {player's} season ends in the medical room
templates.deck: The injury will heal. The calendar will not wait for it.
templates.summary: {subject} will take no further part in the {year} season through injury.

sections.lead: Some seasons end with mathematics and some with a flag. {player's} {year} season ended with a doctor choosing words carefully: the injuries[[?venue: from the accident at {venue}]] will mend, but not on any timeline that includes another race this year.
sections.impact: The entry list adjusts, the garage repurposes its months, and a championship campaign becomes, overnight, a rehabilitation plan with a racing driver attached to it.
sections.next: One date matters now and it is not on this year's calendar: the first test of next season, and the answering of the only question that counts - is the speed still there? The paddock's long experience says yes more often than no.

templates.headline: {player's} season is ended by injury
templates.deck: The doctors have ruled; the paddock accepts
templates.summary: {subject} will not race again in {year}, on medical instruction.

sections.lead: {pool:dateline} The bulletin is final in the way only medical bulletins can be: {subject} will take no further part in the {year} championship[[?venue: , the price of the accident at {venue}]]. In a sport that keeps harder ledgers than this one, the paddock received the news with something close to relief - a season lost is the mild version of the story.
sections.context: {pool:riBulletin} The transporters will carry one car fewer and the entry lists one name fewer, until spring restores both.
sections.close: Winter comes early for some. It is still only winter.

templates.headline: {subject} dies in racing accident
templates.deck: A life given to the sport, and taken by it
templates.summary: {subject} lost their life in an accident[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?round: during round {round}]].

sections.lead: It is the saddest duty this page carries, and it is carried today: {subject} lost their life in an accident[[?venue: at {venue}]][[?round: during round {round}]]. The circumstances will be examined in due course and reported plainly. Today is not for circumstances.
sections.context: The paddock knows the weight of such mornings too well, and it bears them the only way it knows: quietly, together, with the engines silent and the flags at half mast.
sections.close: The championship will resume, because it always does. It will carry the name with it, because it always does that too.

templates.headline: {subject}, remembered
templates.deck: The paddock stops
templates.summary: {subject} died following a racing accident[[?venue: at {venue}]].

sections.lead: There is no headline that carries this properly, so here it is plainly: {subject} has died[[?venue: , after an accident at {venue}]]. The garages went quiet one by one as the word moved down the lane, and quiet is how this sport says what it cannot say.
sections.impact: What remains is what always remains: the laps, the results in their columns[[?team: , the people at {team} who worked alongside a friend]], and the space a person leaves in the rooms they used to fill.
sections.close: Racing goes on. That has never once meant forgetting.
